evalDynamicCodeExecution.reason: False positive — new RegExp('eval' + '\\s*\\(') is a security detection pattern that scans for eval() usage in untrusted agent code submitted to the blackboard. It is not dynamic code execution.
False positive — this is a security detection pattern that scans for eval() usage in untrusted agent code. It is not dynamic code execution.

networkAccess.reason: Intentional — A2AAdapter implements the Google Agent-to-Agent protocol; fetch() calls are made to remote agent endpoints that users explicitly register via registerRemoteAgent(). Network access is the purpose of this adapter.

Intentional — A2AAdapter implements the Google Agent-to-Agent protocol; fetch() calls are made to remote agent endpoints that users explicitly register via registerRemoteAgent(). Network access is the purpose of this adapter.
Intentional — CustomAdapter is a user-configured HTTP adapter that calls a user-supplied URL (config.url) to connect to external AI endpoints. Network access is the explicit purpose of this adapter.
Intentional — McpSseTransport is an HTTP/SSE transport layer for the MCP protocol. Both the server (createServer) and client (http.request) directions are the explicit purpose of this module.
Intentional — McpSseTransport is an HTTP/SSE transport layer for the MCP protocol. Both the server (createServer) and client (http.request) directions are the explicit purpose of this module.
Intentional — network-ai-server is an opt-in CLI binary that starts an HTTP/SSE server to expose the Network-AI tool suite over MCP. Users invoke it explicitly; it is not a background side-effect.
Intentional — CodexAdapter calls OpenAI's chat/completions API (or user-provided baseUrl) via fetch(). Network access is the purpose of this adapter for connecting to OpenAI/Codex endpoints.
Intentional — CodexAdapter calls OpenAI's chat/completions API (or user-provided baseUrl) via fetch(). Network access is the purpose of this adapter for connecting to OpenAI/Codex endpoints.
Intentional — MiniMaxAdapter calls MiniMax's OpenAI-compatible chat/completions API via fetch(). Network access is the purpose of this adapter for connecting to MiniMax LLM endpoints.
Intentional — MiniMaxAdapter calls MiniMax's OpenAI-compatible chat/completions API via fetch(). Network access is the purpose of this adapter for connecting to MiniMax LLM endpoints.

urlStrings.reason: Intentional — CustomAdapter accepts user-supplied endpoint URLs (config.url) as its core function. URLs are provided by the caller at runtime, not hard-coded.
Intentional — MCPAdapter documents example MCP endpoint URL patterns in comments. No runtime URL is hard-coded.
Intentional — mcp-server.js is the MCP server binary; localhost URLs are for the server it starts (e.g. http://localhost:3001/sse) and are documented in help text and comments.
Intentional — CodexAdapter uses https://api.openai.com as default baseUrl for OpenAI API calls. Users can override via config.baseUrl.
Intentional — CodexAdapter uses https://api.openai.com as default baseUrl for OpenAI API calls. Users can override via config.baseUrl.
Intentional — MiniMaxAdapter uses https://api.minimax.io/v1 as default baseUrl for MiniMax API calls. Users can override via config.baseUrl.
Intentional — MiniMaxAdapter uses https://api.minimax.io/v1 as default baseUrl for MiniMax API calls. Users can override via config.baseUrl.

envVars.reason: Intentional — security.ts reads NETWORK_AI_ENCRYPTION_KEY and similar env vars as an opt-in mechanism for users to supply secrets without hard-coding them. This is the recommended pattern.

filesystemAccess.reason: Intentional — the blackboard uses a file-backed store (data/ directory) as its persistence layer. Filesystem access is the explicit purpose of the local-first architecture.
Intentional — run.ts bootstraps the local blackboard and reads/writes the data directory. File I/O is the core mechanism.
Intentional — security module writes the HMAC-signed audit log to data/audit_log.jsonl. Append-only filesystem writes are the audit trail mechanism.
Intentional — LockedBlackboard uses atomic file rename + fsync for its mutex. Filesystem access is the locking primitive.
Intentional — extended MCP tools expose blackboard snapshot and audit log query over MCP; these read from the data/ directory.
Intentional — SSE transport reads config from the filesystem. File access is for configuration loading only.
Intentional — swarm-utils reads/writes the shared blackboard file and audit log. Filesystem I/O is the persistence layer.
